extractions.title: Birth of Yosef Yitzchak Schneersohn
extractions.description: Rabbi Yosef Yitzchak Schneersohn (the Rayatz) is born in Lubavitch. At his circumcision, his grandfather predicts he will grow up to be a Rebbe.

extractions.title: Assassination of Alexander II
extractions.description: Czar Alexander II of Russia is assassinated. Alexander III rolls back reforms and increases oppression of Russian Jews.

extractions.title: Birth of Nechama Dina Schneersohn
extractions.description: Nechama Dina Schneersohn, the future wife of the Rayatz, is born in Kishinev.

extractions.title: Institution of the May Laws
extractions.description: Czar Alexander III institutes the May Laws, heavily restricting Jewish residency, economic growth, and education.

extractions.title: BILU Settlement Begins
extractions.description: The early Russian Zionist group BILU sends its first settlers to Eretz Yisrael.

extractions.title: Rabbi Shmuel Predicts His Passing
extractions.description: Rabbi Shmuel implies to his wife that his passing is imminent, telling her he will 'rise up thirty-two steps in a spiritual way'.

extractions.title: Passing of Rabbi Shmuel (Maharash)
extractions.description: Three days after Yom Kippur, Rabbi Shmuel passes away in Lubavitch at age forty-eight.

extractions.title: Leadership Conclave in Lubavitch
extractions.description: Chabad elders convene and implore the Rebbe's sons to assume leadership. They initially refuse but consent to recite maamarim privately.

extractions.title: Wedding of Uncle Mendel
extractions.description: Two-year-old Yosef Yitzchak watches his uncle Mendel's wedding. During mourning, his mother brings him to his father's study where he is moved by intense praying.

extractions.title: Rashab Returns from Medical Treatment
extractions.description: The Rashab returns to Lubavitch after seeking medical treatment, so changed that young Yosef Yitzchak doesn't recognize him.

extractions.title: Beginning of Education
extractions.description: At age four, Yosef Yitzchak begins formal education under Rabbi Yekusiel Aizik.

extractions.title: Rashbatz Relocates to Bulharkov
extractions.description: Rabbi Shmuel Betzalel Sheftel (the Rashbatz), future teacher of the Rayatz, relocates to Bulharkov.

extractions.title: Move to Yalta for Medical Treatment
extractions.description: Five-year-old Yosef Yitzchak travels with parents to Yalta for six months, taught by Rabbi Schneur Zalman Slonim.

extractions.title: Lag BaOmer Gathering in Kharkiv
extractions.description: During a large crowded dinner where the Rashab delivers a maamar, Yosef Yitzchak notices his mother crying and informs his father.

extractions.title: Studies with Rabbi Shimshon Begin
extractions.description: At age seven, Yosef Yitzchak begins studying with Rabbi Shimshon, memorizing two orders of Mishnah.

extractions.title: Period of Loneliness
extractions.description: The Rashab and wife frequently seek medical treatment. Yosef Yitzchak finds solace with elderly Chasidim and begins recording chasidic lore.

extractions.title: Writing of the Ethical Will
extractions.description: The Rashab writes an ethical will at age twenty-seven outlining how his wife should educate their son if he were to pass away.

extractions.title: Passing of Samuel Polyakov
extractions.description: Samuel Polyakov, the 'rail king of Russia,' passes away.

extractions.title: Rashab Returns to Lubavitch
extractions.description: The Rashab and wife return to Lubavitch. Nine-year-old Yosef Yitzchak feels renewed maturity and begins facing his father during maamarim.

extractions.title: Rashab Begins Yechidut
extractions.description: The Rashab officially begins to receive Chasidim for yechidut (private meetings).

extractions.title: Rashab Spends Full Year at Home
extractions.description: For the first time in years, the Rashab spends the entire year at home in Lubavitch.

extractions.title: Yosef Yitzchak's Prayer at the Ohel
extractions.description: The ten-year-old sneaks out in a snowstorm to the Ohel to pray for his father's recovery. The fever miraculously breaks.

extractions.title: Rashab's Illness (Pneumonia)
extractions.description: The Rashab contracts pneumonia and is dangerously ill for six weeks, asking Yosef Yitzchak to stay by his side.

extractions.title: Yarmarka and Arrest
extractions.description: During the yarmarka, Yosef Yitzchak operates a microloan fund. After defending a butcher, he is wrongfully arrested but released.

extractions.title: Establishment of Loan Fund
extractions.description: Yosef Yitzchak establishes a loan fund for local merchants.

extractions.title: Rashab Studies with Son
extractions.description: The Rashab formally begins studying chasidic philosophy with Yosef Yitzchak.

extractions.title: Passing of Max Lilienthal
extractions.description: Max Lilienthal, German Enlightenment activist who attempted to secularize Jewish education in Russia, passes away.

extractions.title: Bar Mitzvah of Yosef Yitzchak
extractions.description: Yosef Yitzchak celebrates his bar mitzvah, memorizing three maamarim and reciting the third by heart at a gathering.

extractions.title: Rashab Accepts Leadership
extractions.description: The Rashab fully accepts the position of Rebbe, taking his father's seat near the holy ark after eleven years.

extractions.title: Appointment of Rashbatz as Teacher
extractions.description: The Rashab appoints Rabbi Shmuel Betzalel Sheftel as Yosef Yitzchak's dedicated teacher.

extractions.title: Raza Relocates to Vitebsk
extractions.description: Rabbi Zalman Aharon (the Raza) relocates from Lubavitch to Vitebsk, deferring leadership to his younger brother.

extractions.title: Death of Alexander III
extractions.description: Czar Alexander III dies and is succeeded by Nicholas II, who continues oppressive anti-Jewish policies.

extractions.title: Summer Mentoring in Bolivke
extractions.description: The Rashab travels to Bolivke, dedicating time to his fifteen-year-old son's development.

extractions.title: The Akedah - Sacred Covenant
extractions.description: The Rashab takes Yosef Yitzchak to the Ohel, transferring responsibility of working for the public good.

extractions.title: Rayatz Begins Communal Work
extractions.description: From that day forward, Yosef Yitzchak formally begins community work as his father's personal secretary.

extractions.title: Conference of Jewish Leaders in Kovno
extractions.description: At just fifteen, the Rayatz is dispatched to Kovno to represent his father at a conference of Jewish religious leaders.

extractions.title: Engagement of the Rayatz and Nechama Dina
extractions.description: The Rayatz becomes engaged to Nechama Dina Schneersohn in Bolivke.

extractions.title: Rashab's Illness and Recovery
extractions.description: The Rashab falls severely ill and travels to Moscow, receiving a bleak prognosis but recovers and lives another twenty-four years.

extractions.title: Mordechai Ben Hillel Visits Lubavitch
extractions.description: Enlightenment activist Mordechai Ben Hillel visits and opens a secular school with 130 students despite the Rashab's opposition.

extractions.title: Wedding of the Rayatz and Nechama Dina
extractions.description: The chuppah takes place on Friday afternoon in the courtyard of the main synagogue in Lubavitch.

extractions.title: Announcement of Tomchei Temimim
extractions.description: At the wedding reception, the Rashab announces his revolutionary plan to open a yeshivah integrating Talmud and chasidic philosophy.

extractions.title: Opening of Tomchei Temimim
extractions.description: Studies officially begin for the first group of yeshivah students under Rabbi Shmuel Gronem.

extractions.title: Students Return from Zembin
extractions.description: Yeshivah students return from Zembin for the High Holidays, impressing visiting Chasidim with their transformation.

extractions.title: Appointment as Yeshivah Director
extractions.description: Eighteen-year-old Yosef Yitzchak is appointed executive director of the yeshivah and begins fundraising.

extractions.title: Naming of Yeshivas Tomchei Temimim
extractions.description: The Rashab officially names the new institution and declares its students will be titled temimim.

extractions.title: Visit to Moscow
extractions.description: The Rayatz visits Moscow, where Eliezer Polyakov shares a story about his brother Samuel receiving a blessing for wealth.

extractions.title: JCA Pledges 1 Million Francs
extractions.description: The Jewish Colonial Association pledges 1 million francs to support Enlightenment schools. The Rashab organizes opposition.

extractions.title: Mission Statement at Simchat Torah
extractions.description: The Rashab compares students to King David's soldiers, charging them with disseminating Chasidism to the broader world.

extractions.title: Successful Lobbying Against Second JCA Allocation
extractions.description: The Rashab and Rayatz organize lobbying that successfully blocks a second allocation of 600,000 francs.

extractions.title: Conclusion of Formal Studies
extractions.description: The Rayatz concludes his formal studies with the Rashbatz.

extractions.title: Opening of Dubrovno Textile Mill
extractions.description: The Rayatz opens a textile mill in Dubrovno providing Shabbat-observant employment for 2000 Jews.

extractions.title: Travel to St. Petersburg
extractions.description: The Rayatz travels to St. Petersburg for communal matters.

extractions.title: Second Arrest
extractions.description: The Rayatz is arrested for the second time due to accusations by teachers at the secular school in Lubavitch.

extractions.title: Russo-Japanese War Begins
extractions.description: War breaks out between Japan and Russia, leading to enlistment of thousands of Jewish soldiers.

extractions.title: Library Collection Efforts
extractions.description: At age twenty-four, the Rayatz writes to relatives in Jerusalem asking them to search for rare books and manuscripts.

extractions.title: Matzah for Soldiers Initiative
extractions.description: The Rashab launches an initiative to provide matzah and Passover supplies to Jewish soldiers on the Far East front.

extractions.title: Covert Meeting in St. Petersburg
extractions.description: The Rayatz participates in a covert meeting of prominent rabbinic leaders to create strategy against Russian pogroms.

extractions.title: Diplomatic Mission to Western Europe
extractions.description: The Rayatz is sent to Western Europe to persuade statesmen and bankers to apply economic pressure on Russia.

extractions.title: Poalei Tzion Attack on Yeshivah
extractions.description: Members of Poalei Tzion march into the yeshivah. Days later, 200 return with weapons, throw stones, and shoot at the yeshivah.

extractions.title: Raid on Rashab's Summer Home
extractions.description: Poalei Tzion raids the Rashab's summer home in Bolivke, brandishing revolvers while the Rayatz's daughters are present.

extractions.title: Arrest for Riots Investigation
extractions.description: The Rayatz is investigated and arrested due to the riots in Lubavitch, though he proves they were not part of the yeshivah.

extractions.title: Arrest in St. Petersburg
extractions.description: The Rayatz is arrested in St. Petersburg due to efforts to exempt rabbis from military service.

extractions.title: Purchase of Beit Romano
extractions.description: The Rashab successfully purchases Beit Romano, a large property in Hebron, laying foundation to establish Toras Emes.

extractions.title: Confidential Rabbinic Meeting
extractions.description: The Rashab organizes a confidential meeting of allied rabbis to strategize for upcoming elections and a national rabbinic conference.

extractions.title: Pre-Conference Meeting in Vilna
extractions.description: Twenty-five leading rabbis convene in Vilna. The Rayatz serves as secretary.

extractions.title: Travel to Germany
extractions.description: The Rayatz travels to Germany to confer with Jewish communal leaders.

extractions.title: Lubavitch Population
extractions.description: The town of Lubavitch has 3,376 residents, including 2,325 Jews.

extractions.title: National Rabbinical Conference
extractions.description: A national Rabbinical Conference is held in St. Petersburg. The Rayatz assists his father throughout.

extractions.title: Imprisonment Regarding Military Exemptions
extractions.description: The Rayatz is imprisoned in St. Petersburg for efforts to exempt young men from military service.

extractions.title: Passing of Rabbi Yitzchak Dovber of Liadi
extractions.description: Rabbi Yitzchak Dovber of Liadi passes away.

extractions.title: Students Sent to Hebron
extractions.description: The Rashab sends a group of outstanding students, led by Rabbi Zalman Havlin, to establish Toras Emes in Hebron.

extractions.title: Toras Emes Purpose Letter
extractions.description: The Rayatz outlines the purpose of the Hebron yeshivah in a letter to Rabbi Havlin.

extractions.title: First of Four Arrests (1911-1916)
extractions.description: The Rayatz experiences the first of four arrests that would occur between 1911 and 1916.

extractions.title: Founding of World Agudath Israel
extractions.description: World Agudath Israel is established at a conference in Katowice, Poland.

extractions.title: Founding of Young Israel Movement
extractions.description: The Young Israel movement is founded.

extractions.title: Synagogue in Kobe, Japan
extractions.description: A synagogue is established by the small Jewish community in Kobe, Japan.

extractions.title: Beilis Affair Begins
extractions.description: The Beilis affair begins, accusing Mendel Beilis of ritual murder in Kiev.

extractions.title: Dispatching Oscar Gruzenberg
extractions.description: The Rashab dispatches the Rayatz to enlist prominent attorney Oscar Gruzenberg for Beilis's defense.

extractions.title: Appointment of Rabbi Dr. Yosef Hertz
extractions.description: Rabbi Dr. Yosef Hertz is appointed the chief rabbi of Britain.

extractions.title: World War I Begins
extractions.description: World War I breaks out, and Germany declares war on Russia.

extractions.title: Passing of Rebbetzin Rivkah
extractions.description: The Rebbe Rayatz's grandmother, Rebbetzin Rivkah Schneersohn, passes away.

extractions.title: Founding of Mizrachi
extractions.description: The religious Zionist organization Mizrachi is launched.

extractions.title: Establishment of the JDC
extractions.description: The American Joint Distribution Committee is established to help Jews overseas.

extractions.title: Released Time Program Begins
extractions.description: The Released Time program for religious instruction begins in Indiana.

extractions.title: Henry Morgenthau Visits Hebron
extractions.description: Henry Morgenthau, the Jewish US ambassador to the Turkish Empire, is permitted to visit the Tomb of the Patriarchs.

extractions.title: Germany Invades Russia
extractions.description: Germany invades Russia.

extractions.title: Departure from Lubavitch
extractions.description: The Rashab and Rayatz depart Lubavitch with their families due to the advancing German army, ending 102 years of Chabad in Lubavitch.

extractions.title: Second Imprisonment in St. Petersburg
extractions.description: The Rayatz is imprisoned in St. Petersburg a second time regarding military exemptions.

extractions.title: Establishment of Yeshivah in Kutaisi
extractions.description: Jewish community leaders in Kutaisi ask the Rashab for help, and he dispatches rabbis to establish a yeshivah for Sephardic Jews.

extractions.title: Bolshevik Revolution
extractions.description: The Bolshevik Revolution takes place in Russia. Czar Nicholas II abdicates and is eventually executed, ending the Pale of Settlement.

extractions.title: Establishment of Yevesektzia
extractions.description: The Yevesektzia (Jewish section of Communist Party) is established to combat religious tradition.

extractions.title: Establishment of Cheka
extractions.description: The Communist government establishes the Cheka secret police.

extractions.title: Simon Yakovshvili Arrives in Rostov
extractions.description: Simon Yakovshvili stops in Rostov for Rosh HaShanah, meets the Rashab, and decides to stay to study Torah.

extractions.title: Agricultural Colonies Conference
extractions.description: Semyon Dimenstein advocates settling Jews in agricultural colonies during conferences.

extractions.title: Founding of Yeshivah Torah Vodaath
extractions.description: Yeshivah Torah Vodaath is founded in Williamsburg, Brooklyn.

extractions.title: Grigory Lulov Joins Cheka
extractions.description: Grigory Lulov joins the Cheka secret police.

extractions.title: Jewish Educational Council Conference
extractions.description: The Yevesektzia holds a conference in Moscow, establishing the First Jewish Educational Council which bans Hebrew and Bible from schools.

extractions.title: Founding of Hebrew Theological College Chicago
extractions.description: The Hebrew Theological College is founded in Chicago.

extractions.title: Bolsheviks Take Control of Rostov
extractions.description: Bolsheviks take control of Rostov, prompting the Rashab to curtail public activities.

extractions.title: Prediction of 'Dark Clouds'
extractions.description: The Rashab summons the Rayatz, predicting 'dark clouds' for 22 years under Communism, prophesying the regime's eventual collapse.

extractions.title: Purim Farbrengen During Police Visit
extractions.description: A farbrengen at the Rashab's home is interrupted by Bolshevik police; the officers leave after the Rashab delivers a maamar.

extractions.title: Rashab Falls Ill with Typhoid
extractions.description: Shortly after Purim, the Rashab falls ill with typhoid.

extractions.title: Passing of the Rashab
extractions.description: The Rashab passes away in Rostov at the age of 59.

extractions.title: Rayatz Accepts Leadership
extractions.description: The Rayatz accepts the position of the Sixth Lubavitcher Rebbe, reciting a maamar to Chasidim.

extractions.title: Arrest by Cheka
extractions.description: The Rayatz is arrested by Cheka officers, interrogated about Judaism, and subsequently released.

extractions.title: Relocation to Rostov
extractions.description: Yeshivas Tomchei Temimim relocates from Kremenchuk to Rostov due to extreme famine.

extractions.title: Typhus Epidemic in Rostov
extractions.description: A typhus epidemic intensifies in Rostov, causing the Rebbe, his mother, and daughters to fall ill.

extractions.title: Show Trial Against Yeshivah
extractions.description: The Yevesektzia stages a show trial against the Lubavitch yeshivah in Rostov, demanding its closure.

extractions.title: Wedding of Chana Gurary
extractions.description: The Rebbe's oldest daughter, Chana, marries Rabbi Shmaryahu Gurary in Rostov.

extractions.title: Warsaw Branch Opens
extractions.description: News arrives of a new branch of the yeshivah opening in Warsaw.

extractions.title: Relocation to Poltava
extractions.description: To escape famine and scrutiny, Yeshivah Tomchei Temimim relocates to Poltava.

extractions.title: Rabbinical Committee Established
extractions.description: The Rebbe establishes the Rabbinical Committee to advocate for religious rights under Soviet law.

extractions.title: Ramash Meets Chaya Mushka
extractions.description: The Ramash meets his future wife, Chaya Mushka, for the first time in Kislovodsk.

extractions.title: Call for Emissaries
extractions.description: The Rebbe asks for volunteers to act as emissaries to establish secret places of Jewish study across the country.

extractions.title: Secret Covenant
extractions.description: In Moscow, the Rebbe formalizes a 'secret covenant' with nine trusted Chasidim, swearing an oath of self-sacrifice for Jewish education.

extractions.title: Flight to Leningrad
extractions.description: Facing imminent threat of arrest, the Rebbe is forced to flee Rostov and relocates to Leningrad.

extractions.title: Founding of Agudas Chassidei Chabad in US
extractions.description: Agudas Chassidei Chabad is established in the United States.

extractions.title: Opposition to National Conference
extractions.description: Lev Gurewicz attempts to enlist the Rebbe to support a national conference. The Rebbe opposes it as a Yevesektzia-backed plot.

extractions.title: Library Acquisition
extractions.description: The Rebbe acquires a massive private library collection containing thousands of rare volumes.

extractions.title: Underground Network Success
extractions.description: A confidential JDC report reveals 15,000 children are being educated in secret schools.

extractions.title: Rabbinical Conference in Korosten
extractions.description: A rabbinical conference occurs in Korosten. The Rebbe sends an emissary to steer the agenda toward tradition.

extractions.title: Narrow Escape from GPU Agents
extractions.description: The Rebbe narrowly evades Yevesektzia agents on a train and in his hotel, saved by a sympathetic GPU official.

extractions.title: Arrest of Secretary
extractions.description: The Rebbe's secretary, Rabbi Elchonon Morozov, is arrested and jailed.

extractions.title: Fiery Farbrengen
extractions.description: The Rebbe leads a fiery farbrengen, urging Chasidim to defy the government and be willing to cast themselves into flames.

extractions.title: Arrest and Death Sentence
extractions.description: Just after midnight, the Rebbe is arrested by GPU officers and taken to Shpalerka prison, sentenced to death by firing squad.

extractions.title: International Outcry and Commutation
extractions.description: Massive international outcry forces the Soviets to commute his sentence to three-year exile in Kostroma.

extractions.title: Release from Exile
extractions.description: The Rebbe is informed of his release from exile and returns to Leningrad two days later.

extractions.title: Hiding in Malakhovka
extractions.description: The Rebbe hides in Malakhovka outside Moscow while exit visas are secured.

extractions.title: Final Holiday in Russia
extractions.description: The Rebbe celebrates his final holiday in Russia, surrounded by thousands of weeping Chasidim.

extractions.title: Departure from Russia
extractions.description: The Rebbe departs Russia by train with his historic library, arriving to a hero's welcome in Riga.

extractions.title: First Maamar in Exile
extractions.description: The Rebbe publishes his first maamar since leaving Russia, printed in Yiddish to broaden dissemination.

extractions.title: Royal Wedding
extractions.description: The 'Royal Wedding' of the Rebbe's daughter Chaya Mushka to the Ramash takes place at the yeshivah in Warsaw.

extractions.title: Matzah Shipping Campaign
extractions.description: The Rebbe orchestrates an international campaign to ship 140,000 kilos of matzah into the Soviet Union.

extractions.title: Journey to Eretz Yisrael
extractions.description: The Rebbe embarks on a year-long journey, first traveling to Eretz Yisrael.

extractions.title: Visit to Jerusalem and Hebron
extractions.description: He visits Jerusalem, meets Chief Rabbis Kook and Sonnenfeld, visits Hebron and Me'arat HaMakhpelah.

extractions.title: Arrival in New York Harbor
extractions.description: The Rebbe arrives in New York Harbor to a crowd of thousands.

extractions.title: Declaration: America is No Different
extractions.description: Celebrating the High Holidays in Brooklyn, he declares his transformative vision: 'America iz nisht aderesh'.

extractions.title: Meeting with Justice Brandeis
extractions.description: The Rebbe travels to Washington to meet with Supreme Court Justice Louis Brandeis about aiding Soviet Jews.

extractions.title: Arrest of Correspondents
extractions.description: Sixteen Jewish leaders are arrested in Minsk for corresponding with the Rebbe.

extractions.title: Visit to St. Louis
extractions.description: The Rebbe visits St. Louis, Missouri, where thousands welcome him and he inspires a mikveh campaign.

extractions.title: Meeting with President Hoover
extractions.description: The Rebbe meets with US President Herbert Hoover at the White House.

extractions.title: Departure from the US
extractions.description: The Rebbe departs the US for Europe.

extractions.title: Recuperation in Marienbad
extractions.description: The Rebbe recovers in Marienbad, meeting with the Belzer and Gerrer Rebbes.

extractions.title: Opposition to Communist Youth Club
extractions.description: The Rebbe confronts members of a Communist youth club plotting to disrupt Simchat Torah.

extractions.title: Visit to Rakshik
extractions.description: The Rebbe visits Rakshik, Lithuania, to give local Chasidim an opportunity to visit him.

extractions.title: Visit to Warsaw
extractions.description: The Rebbe travels to Warsaw to survey the yeshivah and meets with students from Lodz and Vilnius.

extractions.title: Letter to the Ramash
extractions.description: The Rebbe writes a lengthy letter to the Ramash detailing Chabad's intellectual and spiritual focus.

extractions.title: Wedding of Sheina Schneersohn
extractions.description: The Rebbe celebrates the marriage of his youngest daughter Sheina to Rabbi Mendel Horenstein.

extractions.title: Medical Treatment in Berlin
extractions.description: The Rebbe spends two months in Berlin for medical care, accompanied by the Ramash.

extractions.title: Ramash and Chaya Mushka Flee Berlin
extractions.description: Fleeing the Nazi threat, the Ramash and Chaya Mushka leave Berlin and resettle in Paris.

extractions.title: Permanent Relocation to Warsaw
extractions.description: After securing Latvian citizenship, the Rebbe permanently relocates to Warsaw, Poland.

extractions.title: Emissary to New York
extractions.description: Rabbi Yitzchak Horowitz arrives in New York as the Rebbe's emissary to strengthen Chabad in America.

extractions.title: School Reform in Latvia
extractions.description: Rabbi Chaim Mordechai Aizek Hodakov is appointed chief inspector and begins reforming the curriculum.

extractions.title: MS Diagnosis
extractions.description: Seeking treatment for illness, the Rebbe travels to Vienna where Dr. Max Gerson diagnoses multiple sclerosis.

extractions.title: Relocation to Otwock
extractions.description: Due to health needs, the Rebbe relocates permanently to Otwock, Poland.

extractions.title: Founding of Achot Hatemimim
extractions.description: The journal Hatomim is founded and the women's organization is created.

extractions.title: Raid on Package Smuggling Operation
extractions.description: Latvian police arrest four Chabad Chasidim running the Rebbe's covert package-smuggling operation.

extractions.title: Arrest of Simon Yakovshvili
extractions.description: Simon Yakovshvili is arrested by secret police, tortured, and sentenced to death before commutation.

extractions.title: Departure from Austria
extractions.description: The Rebbe departs Austria for Paris just three days before Germany annexes Austria.

extractions.title: US Students Enroll
extractions.description: Students from the US begin enrolling in the Rebbe's yeshivah in Otwock.

extractions.title: Execution of Elchonon Morozov
extractions.description: The Rebbe's secretary is captured and executed by firing squad.

extractions.title: Germany Invades Poland
extractions.description: Germany invades Poland and World War II begins. Otwock is bombed.

extractions.title: Evacuation to Warsaw
extractions.description: The Rebbe and family are evacuated to Warsaw in a Latvian Embassy vehicle.

extractions.title: Escape to Riga
extractions.description: Admiral Wilhelm Canaris rescues the Rebbe and family; they arrive safely in Riga.

extractions.title: US Visas Granted
extractions.description: The US Consulate in Riga issues visas to the Rebbe and his entourage.

extractions.title: Departure from Riga
extractions.description: The Rebbe departs Riga by plane to Stockholm.

extractions.title: Boarding the SS Drottningholm
extractions.description: The Rebbe boards the SS Drottningholm in Gothenburg, Sweden.

extractions.title: Arrival in New York Harbor
extractions.description: The Drottningholm arrives in New York Harbor. The Rebbe famously declares 'America iz nisht aderesh'.

extractions.title: Opening of American Yeshivah
extractions.description: The day after arrival, the Rebbe announces the opening of Yeshivas Tomchei Temimim in America.

extractions.title: Released Time Program Approved
extractions.description: NY Governor signs statute approving the Released Time program. The Rebbe immediately harnesses this.

extractions.title: Purchase of 770 Eastern Parkway
extractions.description: Agudas Chassidei Chabad purchases 770 Eastern Parkway as the Rebbe's permanent residence.

extractions.title: Move into 770
extractions.description: The Rebbe moves into his permanent headquarters at 770 Eastern Parkway in Crown Heights.

extractions.title: Ramash and Chaya Mushka Flee Paris
extractions.description: After fleeing Paris to Vichy, they board a ship in Lisbon.

extractions.title: Safe Arrival in New York
extractions.description: The Ramash and Chaya Mushka arrive safely in New York.

extractions.title: Pearl Harbor Attack
extractions.description: As Pearl Harbor is attacked, Rabbi Mottel Altein is traveling by train to establish a Hebrew school.

extractions.title: Anguished Letter about Holocaust
extractions.description: The Rebbe writes an anguished letter lamenting the silence regarding the Holocaust.

extractions.title: Murder of Sheina Horenstein
extractions.description: The Rebbe's youngest daughter is murdered in the Treblinka death camp.

extractions.title: Murder of Mendel Horenstein
extractions.description: The Rebbe's son-in-law is murdered in Treblinka.

extractions.title: Montreal Yeshivah Building Dedicated
extractions.description: A dedicated building is purchased for the growing Chabad yeshivah in Montreal.

extractions.title: Passing of Ramash's Father
extractions.description: The Ramash's father, Rabbi Levi Yitzchak Schneerson, passes away in exile in Kazakhstan.

extractions.title: Heart Attack After Riga Report
extractions.description: The Rebbe suffers a heart attack after receiving a report on the Nazi massacre of Jews in Riga.

extractions.title: Relief Office in Paris
extractions.description: A Chabad relief office is established in Paris to assist refugees.

extractions.title: The Great Escape Begins
extractions.description: Chabad Chasidim begin secretly crossing the border from Lvov to Poland using forged Polish passports.

extractions.title: Reunion with Mother
extractions.description: The Ramash travels to Paris to reunite with his mother after 20 years of separation.

extractions.title: Massive Transport Crosses Border
extractions.description: A massive transport of 232 Chasidim successfully crosses the Russian border. The Rebbe senses their crossing spiritually.

extractions.title: End of Great Escape
extractions.description: The Great Escape ends when Soviet authorities arrest escape organizers in Lvov.

extractions.title: Founding of Kfar Chabad
extractions.description: Following the establishment of the State of Israel, the Rebbe directs the founding of Kfar Chabad.

extractions.title: Israel's First Elections
extractions.description: Israel holds its first elections. The Rebbe instructs Chasidim to participate and vote.

extractions.title: US Citizenship Granted
extractions.description: The Rebbe and his family are granted US citizenship at 770 Eastern Parkway.

extractions.title: Plan for North African Education
extractions.description: The Rebbe meets with Yitzchak Levy to plan sending teachers to North Africa.

extractions.title: Final Active Period
extractions.description: In days leading up to his passing, the Rebbe is highly active, dispatching letters globally and holding yechidut.

extractions.title: Passing of the Rayatz
extractions.description: At 7:45 a.m. on Shabbat morning, the Rebbe suffers a final heart attack and passes away at 7:50 a.m.

extractions.title: Funeral of the Rayatz
extractions.description: Thousands crowd Eastern Parkway for the Rebbe's funeral. He is interred at Old Montefiore Cemetery in Queens.
